title: Grinding and Finishing
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Grinding and Finishing process used to improve the surface quality, dimensional accuracy, and appearance of stainless steel products before final inspection and dispatch.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of Grinding and Finishing.
Identify the equipment used during Grinding.

content.introduction: Grinding and Finishing are the final surface improvement processes before quality inspection. These operations remove minor surface defects, improve appearance, and achieve the required surface finish according to customer specifications.
content.purpose: The primary objective of Grinding is to remove surface imperfections and provide the required finish, smoothness, and dimensional accuracy.
content.workingPrinciple: The stainless steel product passes through grinding machines equipped with abrasive belts or grinding wheels. Controlled material removal produces a smooth and uniform surface finish.
safetyPrecautions: Wear safety glasses and face shield.
Keep hands away from moving belts.
Ensure dust extraction system is operating properly.
content.summary: Grinding and Finishing improve the appearance and quality of stainless steel products by removing surface imperfections and achieving the specified finish before final quality inspection.
